what_it_does: Stores a posted note/comment by Tim that replies to another noticeboard item, expressing that he does not understand a described pattern and cautioning that any principle attributed to him is not final.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks this as a note with id item-bbd02d97, source claude_code, author_session tim, channel dragnet-development.
It links to another item via commented_on target board://item-939d7822.
Body opens with [point] and responds to a claim about cards, observation-threads, and living/accumulating understanding.
Tim states he has not seen cards, is not in the channel, and has not written or reviewed the relevant code.
Tim says the identified principle may be something of his that got built, but should be assumed not final, finished, or correct.
standouts: Direct skepticism from the person whose principle is being referenced.
Explicit caveat that built principles are provisional/not canonical.
A meta-commentary on a design discussion rather than a technical artifact.
concerns: Author files a substantive reaction while explicitly lacking the surrounding context, which may make the note hard to interpret without the parent item.
notes: Tim's context-limited, cautionary reply to a design point, reminding readers that attributed principles are still provisional.
questions: What is the content of the parent item-939d7822?
What are 'cards' and the 'dragnet observation' model in this project?
What specific principle of Tim's is being referenced?
What does 'mark-is-truth' mean in this codebase?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a posted note/comment; actual content is a disclaimer and caution about a design pattern, which fits the comment form.
intention_signals: To push back on overconfidence in a surfaced pattern and remind the channel that any built principle remains unfinished and open to revision.
novelty: A principle-originator explicitly undermining the apparent solidity of a pattern attributed to them.
contribution: Captures a dissenting or provisional stance and a context boundary for a design discussion; preserves the author's explicit caveat that principles should not be treated as final.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on item-939d7822, stating he lacks context to understand a proposed 'living understanding' pattern and cautioning that any built principle is not final.
intention_for_embedding: To register skepticism and a 'not final' caveat on a design pattern attributed to the author's prior work.
